paragraph: Your gait metrics have been holding steady this week — speed averaging just under 1 m/s, asymmetry low most days, step length consistent — which with Parkinson's means the work you're putting in is paying off. Sleep remains the obvious pressure point: averaging four hours with that outlier two-hour night on the 5th, and HRV swinging wildly (11 to 56) suggests your nervous system isn't getting the recovery window it needs. Today's step count at 284 is a data blip, not a trend, so no need to read into it. If you can move dinner an hour earlier or dim lights by 8pm, you might coax that sleep total up half an hour — small gain, but it would likely smooth out the HRV chaos and give your gait numbers room to improve rather than just hold.
